verses.text: <sc>The</sc> Senior to the Lady Elect and her children, whom I love in truth, and not I only, but also all that have known the truth,
for the truth which abideth in us, and shall be with us for ever.
Grace be with you, mercy, peace from God the Father, and from Christ <sc>Jesus</sc> the Son of the Father in truth, and charity.
Iwas exceeding glad, because I have found of thy children walking in truth, as we have received commandment of the Father.
And now I beseech thee Lady, not as writing a new commandment to thee, but that which we have had from the beginning, <cr>[1]</cr> that we love one another.

And this is charity, that we walk according to his commandments. For this is the commandment, that as 6. From the beginning.] ◦ This is the rule of a Christian Catholic man, to walk in the faith and worship of God which he hath received from the beginning. Which is that which we now call according to the Scriptures, the tradition of the Apostles : that which is come to us from man to man, from Bishop to Bishop, and so from the Apostles. So shall a faithful man avoid seducers that rise up in every age, teaching new doctrine. ◦ To hold fast the old received faith. you have heard from the beginning, you walk in the same:
because many seducers are gone out into the world, which do not confess <sc>Jesus</sc> Christ to have come into flesh: this is a seducer and an Antichrist.
Look to yourselves, that you lose not the things which you have wrought: but that you may receive a full <cr>[1]</cr> reward. <cr>[1]</cr> Reward for keeping fast the Catholic faith.

Every one that <cr>[1]</cr> revolteth, and persisteth not in the doctrine of Christ, hath not God. He that persisteth in the doctrine, the same hath both the Father, and the Son. <cr>[1]</cr> c To go back or revolt from the received truth and doctrine Apostolical, is damnable.
If [1] any man come to you, and bring not this doctrine, receive him not into the house, nor say, God save you , unto him.
For he that saith unto him, God save you , communicateth with his wicked works.
Having more things to write unto you: I would not by paper and ink: for I hope that I shall be with you, and speak mouth to mouth: that your joy may be full.

The children of thy sister Elect salute thee. 10. This doctrine.] ◦ The Apostles, and true Pastors their lawful successors, and the Church of God in holy Councel, use to set down the true doctrine in those points which Heretics call into controversy. Which being once done and declared to the faithful, they need no other mark or description to know an Heretic or false teacher by, but that he cometh with another doctrine than that which is set down to them. Neither can the Heretics shift themselves, as now a days they would do, saying, o let us first be proved Heretics by the Scriptures, let them define an Heretic. No, this is not the Apostle's rule. Many a good honest shepherd knoweth a wolf, that cannot define him. but the Apostle saith, If he bring not this set doctrine, he is a seducer. So holy Church saith now, Christ is really in the B. Sacrament, under form of bread and wine &c. If therefore he bring not this doctrine, he is a seducer, and an Heretic and we must avoid him, whether in his own definitions and censures he seem to himself an Heretic or no. 10. Receive him not.] ◦ Though in such times and places where the community or most part be infected, necessity often forceth the faithful to converse with such in worldly affairs, to salute them, to eat and speak with them, and the Church by decree of Councel, for the more quietness of timorous consciences provideth, that they incur not excommunication or other censures for communicating in worldly affairs with any in this kind, except they be by name excommunicated or declared to be Heretics: yet even in worldly conversation and secular acts of our life, we must avoid them as much as we may, because their familiarity is many ways contagious and noisome to good men, namely to the simple: but in matter of religion, in praying, reading their books, hearing their sermons, presence at their service, partaking of their Sacraments, and all other communicating with them in spiritual thins, it is a great damnable sin to deal with them. 10. Nor say, God save you.] [1] S. Irenaeus ( li. 3. c. 3. ) reporteth a notable story of this holy Apostle touching this point, out of S. Polycarpus, which is this. There be some (saith he) that have heard Polycarpe say, that when John the disciple of our Lord was going to Ephesus, into a bath, to wash himself, and saw Cerinthus the Heretic within the same, he suddenly skipt out, saying that he feared lest the bath should fall, because Cerinthus the enemy of truth was within . So saith he of S. John, and addeth also a like worthy example of [2] S. Polycarpe himself: who on a time meeting Marcion the Heretic, and the said Marcion calling upon him, and asking him whether he knew him not: Yes , quoth Polycarpe, I know thee for Satan's son and heir. So great fear (saith S. Irenaeus) had the Apostles and their disciples to communicate in word only, with such as were adulterers or corrupters of the truth. as S. Paul also warned, when he said, [3] A man that is an Heretic, after the first and second admonition avoid . So far Irenaeus. If then, to speak with them or salute them, is so earnestly to be avoided according to this Apostle's example & doctrine: what a sin is it to flatter them, to serve them, to marry with them, and so forth? ◦ To bring willfully another doctrine than the Catholic Church setteth down, is always a mark of seducers & Heretics. ◦ When & wherein to converse with Heretics, is tolerable, when & wherein, it is damnable. [1] S. John would not be in one bath with Cerinthus the Heretic. [2] The like zeal of S. Polycarp, and other Apostolic men in not communicating with Heretics.

chapters.summary: He commendeth the lady and her sons for continuing in the old faith, bidding them so to do hereafter also, lest they lose the reward of their works in the day of judgement: and to love the true believers, but with Heretics to have no society: expressing also the points then in controversy.
